name: @DEATHSTAR Compute Doctrine
description: The @HOMELAB IS one virtual cluster called ULTRON — the Death Star. All nodes present as a single compute surface. Cloud is overflow only. Job scheduling inspired by mainframe JCL + modern chip pipeline architecture.
core_principles: LOCAL-FIRST: All workloads run on ULTRON cluster by default
CLOUD = OVERFLOW: Azure/third-party frameworks absorb burst only when ULTRON saturated
ONE VIRTUAL CLUSTER: All homelab nodes present as 'ULTRON' — one compute surface
BALANCED WORKTREES: @GRID load-balances across nodes, no single point of saturation
MAINFRAME DISCIPLINE: Jobs are declared, scheduled, and tracked like mainframe JCL
CHIP PIPELINE: Workflows flow through fetch→decode→execute→memory→writeback stages

cloud_overflow.description: External compute absorbed ONLY when ULTRON cluster saturated

azure.overflow_trigger: Service Bus queue depth > 100 OR Azure Functions for serverless burst

anthropic.overflow_trigger: Local models insufficient for task complexity

cloud_overflow.policy: NEVER migrate primary workloads to cloud. Cloud is burst buffer, not home base.

mainframe_jcl_model.description: Workflows modeled after IBM mainframe Job Control Language (JCL). Every unit of work is a JOB with STEPs, each STEP has an EXEC (program) and DD (data definitions).

A.name: CRITICAL
A.routing: Nearest available core with GPU preference

B.name: HIGH
B.description: Trading signals, real-time analysis, user-initiated tasks

D.name: LOW
D.description: Background maintenance, log rotation, health checks
D.routing: NAS or BitNet preferred (off-load GPU cores)

E.name: BATCH
E.description: Long-running batch jobs, model training, mass analysis
E.routing: KAIJU (Iron Legion) for GPU, NAS for CPU-bound

job_lifecycle.description: Mainframe JES2 (Job Entry Subsystem) inspired lifecycle

states.description: Waiting in priority queue per job class

step_template.description: Each STEP in a JOB follows this pattern

fields.DD_SYSIN: Standard input source (previous step output / cache / config)
fields.COND: Condition codes — proceed/skip based on prior step RC
fields.REGION: Resource allocation (GPU memory, CPU cores, max runtime)

wopr_mainframe_array.description: @WOPR is a MAINFRAME for PATTERNS — a custom @ARRAY using Jupyter Notebooks (Holocrons) with database linking, embedding, and @JOCASTA_NU as research librarian
wopr_mainframe_array.analogy: IBM z/Series + Sysplex for pattern processing

array_nodes.description: The @WOPR array is distributed across ULTRON cluster, with NAS as primary DASD

embedding_store.purpose: Semantic search over patterns, strategies, intel reports
embedding_store.sync: rsync every 4h to NAS, Azure Blob for DR

simulation_engine.purpose: Pattern fitness evaluation via simulated market scenarios

jocasta_nu.description: @JOCASTA_NU — Jedi Archivist and Research Librarian of the @WOPR mainframe
jocasta_nu.role: Research, curation, and retrieval of pattern knowledge
responsibilities: Index and catalog all pattern discoveries across Holocron notebooks
Link patterns to their source intel (which @SYPHON sweep found them)
Maintain embedding coherency between ChromaDB and MariaDB
Curate the Jedi Archives (NAS Jupyter) — prune stale, promote validated
Cross-reference patterns against historical market data
Serve as RAG retrieval interface for JARVIS and subagents
Flag pattern drift — when live performance diverges from backtest
data_flow: @WOPR simulation → fitness scores → @JOCASTA_NU links to source patterns
Live trading → detection logs → @JOCASTA_NU tracks drift
Any agent → RAG query → @JOCASTA_NU retrieves relevant knowledge

modern_chip_pipeline.description: Extends homelab_cpu_architecture.json with modern out-of-order execution concepts

superscalar.description: Multiple execution units running in parallel (like modern CPUs)
superscalar.note: Up to 4 jobs executing simultaneously across local ports. Port 4 (cloud) only when ports 0-3 saturated.

out_of_order_execution.description: Jobs can complete out of order if no data dependencies
out_of_order_execution.note: @C-3PO tracks job dependencies. Independent jobs execute on any available port regardless of submission order.

speculative_execution.description: Start likely-needed work before confirmed (like CPU branch prediction)
use_cases: @SYPHON pre-fetches intel sources before sweep is formally triggered
@WOPR pre-warms simulation parameters based on last market regime
Cache service pre-loads compiled memory before session fully starts
speculative_execution.rollback: If speculative work proves unnecessary, discard silently

simd.description: Single Instruction, Multiple Data — batch operations
use_cases: Strategy Collider runs 7 particle types through same fitness function
@DYNO stress tests 8 profiles against same strategy simultaneously
@SYPHON sweeps 7 sources in parallel with same query template

branch_prediction.misprediction_penalty: Flush speculative results, re-execute on correct path

BATCH.routing: Least-loaded core, prefer NAS for CPU-bound

c3po_dispatcher.description: @C-3PO is the central job dispatcher — the JES2 of the @DEATHSTAR
c3po_dispatcher.role: Manager of @HELPDESK, routes all jobs through proper channels
responsibilities: Accept job submissions from all entry points (CLI, API, n8n, Azure Bus)
Classify job class (A-E) based on content and urgency
Route to appropriate @GRID node based on WLM service class
Track job lifecycle (INPUT → EXECUTION → OUTPUT → PURGE)
Escalate to JARVIS when job class A (CRITICAL) triggered
Coordinate with @GRID heartbeat for node health awareness
Manage job dependencies (hold STEP 2 until STEP 1 RC=0)

overflow_policy.description: When to spill compute to cloud — the @GRID governor decides

triggers.gpu_saturation: All GPU ports busy for > 30s with queued jobs
triggers.complexity_threshold: Task requires capabilities beyond local models (e.g., 200K+ context)
triggers.explicit_request: User or JARVIS explicitly requests cloud execution

cloud_budget.monthly_cap: Defined per provider in Azure Key Vault
